commands.review_summary:
The PR rewrites the v2026.6.11 changelog section, adds a dedicated Mintlify release-notes page, and links it from the release index and docs navigation.

PR surface: Docs +121. Total +121 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is release documentation publication, not a runtime bug. Source inspection shows current main still has the release-index placeholder and no dedicated v2026.6.11 page.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is appropriate because this maintainer-labeled draft release publication needs release/editorial confirmation and docs validation, not an autonomous repair lane.

target.title: docs: publish release notes for v2026.6.11
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Resolves a problem where the published v2026.6.11 changelog and release-note surfaces do not yet use the human-approved public release story or the docs release page as the canonical full-notes destination.

## Why This Change Was Made

This publishes the approved v2026.6.11 public changelog section and adds the matching Mintlify release-notes page under `docs/releases/2026.6.11.md`. The docs release page becomes the full-notes target, while the GitHub release body remains a separate post-merge update so its live release verification block can be preserved exactly.

## User Impact

Readers get a clearer v2026.6.11 release story focused on rough edges from real use, including misplaced replies, stuck sends, reconnects, model setup failures, and safer admin defaults. Technical readers still get linked source and contributor detail without making the public changelog read like raw maintainer accounting.

## Evidence

- Human review approved release-notes eval iteration 10.
- The approved iteration is preserved under `evals/v2026.6.11/iterations/10/outputs/`.
- Structural generation validation passed for the approved artifact set.
- Independent editorial evaluation passed with zero findings.
- Publication comparison is approved with one intentional warning about replacing the previous raw contribution-heavy changelog section with the approved public-vs-maintainer boundary.
target.author: hannesrudolph
